title: UX Polish
description: Polish the Activity timeline with date group separators, gate GoalProgress SVG animation on prefers-reduced-motion, add an Edit Goal shortcut, add a sort toggle to Tags, add a live region for calendar month announcements, show an active filter dot on the mobile saved searches button, add a visual progress bar to OnboardingChecklist, and add a caption to the OfferComparison table.

userStories.title: Add date group separators to Activity timeline
userStories.description: Activity.jsx renders all timeline entries in a flat list with no indication of which day they belong to. Group entries by calendar date and render a sticky or inline date separator heading (e.g., 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'May 2, 2026') between groups.
acceptanceCriteria: Activity.jsx groups timeline entries by calendar date
A date separator element renders between groups showing the date label (Today / Yesterday / formatted date for older entries)
Separator is visually distinct (e.g., small muted text with a horizontal rule or background)
Entries within each group retain their existing styling
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Gate GoalProgress SVG ring transition on prefers-reduced-motion
userStories.description: GoalProgress.jsx applies an inline transition style [transition:stroke-dashoffset_0.4s_ease] to the SVG ring unconditionally. Gate this animation so it only runs when the user has not requested reduced motion.
acceptanceCriteria: GoalProgress.jsx checks window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches
When reduced motion is preferred, the stroke-dashoffset transition style is not applied
When reduced motion is not preferred, the transition runs as before
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add Edit Goal shortcut button to GoalProgress
userStories.description: GoalProgress.jsx displays the goal ring and stats but provides no way to edit the goal directly. Add a small 'Edit goal' link/button that navigates to /settings?section=pipeline so users can quickly adjust their goal.
acceptanceCriteria: GoalProgress.jsx renders an 'Edit goal' button or link below or near the goal display
Clicking it navigates to /settings?section=pipeline (use react-router-dom Link or useNavigate)
The button is visually understated (e.g., text-xs text-muted-foreground underline or a ghost Button)
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add sort toggle to Tags page
userStories.description: Tags.jsx renders a flat list of tags with no sorting control. Add a sort toggle in the page header area (Name A→Z / Count ↓) that reorders the displayed tags.
acceptanceCriteria: Tags.jsx has a sort control with at least two options: sort by name (A-Z) and sort by count (descending)
The currently active sort is visually indicated (e.g., aria-pressed or active styling)
Switching sort reorders the tag list in the UI without a page reload
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-live region for Calendar month navigation
userStories.description: Calendar.jsx changes the displayed month but never announces the change to screen readers. Add an aria-live='polite' region that announces the new month/year string whenever navigation occurs.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar.jsx (or CalendarGrid.jsx) has an aria-live='polite' element that is updated with the current month and year on every navigation
The announcement element is visually hidden (sr-only) so it doesn't affect layout
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Show active filter dot on mobile Saved Searches button in JobBoard
userStories.description: JobBoard.jsx mobile 'Saved searches' button has no indicator of how many saved searches exist. Add a small badge or dot on the button when there are saved searches available, giving users a visual cue to open the panel.
acceptanceCriteria: JobBoard.jsx mobile 'Saved searches' button shows a small numeric badge or dot indicator when saved searches exist (count > 0)
The indicator is hidden when there are no saved searches
Use the useSavedSearches hook or equivalent to get the count
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add visual progress bar to OnboardingChecklist
userStories.description: OnboardingChecklist.jsx shows steps as a list but has no overall progress indicator. Add a simple progress bar (or '2 of 3 steps complete' text) at the top of the checklist to motivate completion.
acceptanceCriteria: OnboardingChecklist.jsx counts completed steps (checked items) and total steps
A progress bar or 'N of M steps complete' label renders above or below the step list
The progress bar uses the project's color tokens (bg-primary for fill, bg-muted for track)
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add caption to OfferComparison comparison table
userStories.description: OfferComparison.jsx renders an offer comparison table without a <caption>. Screen reader users cannot identify the table's purpose. Add <caption className='sr-only'>Offer comparison</caption> as the first child of the table.
acceptanceCriteria: OfferComparison.jsx comparison <table> has <caption className='sr-only'>Offer comparison</caption> as its first child
Build passes with no errors
